zh.Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔
zh.Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可
zh.Knockin, Oswestry, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔
zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger le Strange於1301年8月15日出生在英國史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔時，他的父親John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin19歲，母親Isolda De Walton24歲。他於1325年與Maud Basset在英國英格蘭史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1342年登記服兵役。他去世於1349年7月29日在英國林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger le Strange于1301年8月15日出生在英国史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔时，他的父亲John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin19岁，母亲Isolda De Walton24岁。他于1325年与Maud Basset在英国英格兰史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1342年登记服兵役。他去世于1349年7月29日在英国林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Lewis   or respelling of   Lou  , as borne by the impresario  Lew Grade  (  1906–98 , originally  Louis Winogradsky ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger le Strange was born on 15 August 1301, in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, his father, John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin, was 19 and his mother, Isolda De Walton, was 24. He married Maud Basset in 1325, in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He registered for military service in 1342. He died on 29 July 1349, in Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger le Strange est né le 15 août 1301, à Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Isolda De Walton, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Maud Basset en 1325, à Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1342. Il est décédé le 29 juillet 1349, à Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans.

Lui sposato Maud Basset nel 1325, nel Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1342. ja.lifeSummary: Roger le Strangeが1301年8月15日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアノクイン イン オスウエストリーで生まれた時，父John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockinは19歳，母Isolda De Waltonは24歳であった。1325年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアノクイン イン オスウエストリーでMaud Bassetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1342年に兵役登録をした。彼は1349年7月29日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアセジェブルークで47歳で死去した。

ko.Knockin, Oswestry, Shropshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 노킨 인 오스w스트리
ko.lifeSummary: Roger le Strange(이)가 1301년 8월 15일 영국 슈럽실르 노킨 인 오스w스트리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Isolda De Walton(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1325년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 노킨 인 오스w스트리에서 Maud Basset(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1342년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1349년 7월 29일 영국 링코실르 세지브룩에서 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger le Strange nasceu em 15 de agosto de 1301, em Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Isolda De Walton, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Basset em 1325, em Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1342. Ele faleceu em 29 de julho de 1349, em Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 47 anos.

ru.Knockin, Oswestry, Shropshire, England: ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger le Strange родился 15 августа 1301 г. в местности ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John le Strange VI 2nd Lord Strange of Knockin было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Isolda De Walton, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Basset в 1325 г. в местности ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1342 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 29 июля 1349 г. в местности седжебрук, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет.

Murió el 29 de julio de 1349, en Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 47 años.
